summary: Backend: 13/13 pytest tests PASS for iteration 7. PUT /api/auth/profile works for admin & testuser (self-service), whitespace-clears-to-null, missing auth → 401, and — critically — locked owner CAN still update profile (deps on get_current_user verified end-to-end by injecting 10 customers via Mongo, confirming /customers POST is 403 while /auth/profile PUT is 200). Regression: admin activate/extend/deactivate, login/me shape, and /customers listing all still OK. testuser and admin profiles restored to their pre-run values in Mongo (best-effort — see notes). Frontend: sign-in form uses testIDs signin-username-input / signin-password-input / signin-submit-button. Playwright automation to walk the lock-screen sign-out flow and admin-profile flow was attempted several times but scripts crashed on Python string-escape issues (loop-prevention triggered). Manual code review + backend evidence indicates the fix is correct: subscription-lock.tsx explicitly router.replace('/sign-in') in the confirm handler and also in a null-user useEffect; admin-profile.tsx has all required testIDs, a role-based useEffect guard, and correct wiring to api.updateProfile / api.changePassword. Web preview loads (Daftari sign-in screen rendered). Seed data cleaned up: V7_LOCK_* customers removed via pymongo.
action_items: Main agent: please self-verify the frontend flows in a live session. Backend contract is confirmed by pytest; the FE fix (router.replace('/sign-in') after signOut in subscription-lock.tsx and the useEffect null-user redirect) is present and reviewed but was not fully exercised by Playwright in this iteration due to test-harness scripting failures (Loop Prevention stopped further UI attempts).

Admin's shop_name/phone were left as 'مشرف دفتري' / '0926609606' after the PUT profile tests (backend pytest restored them, then the UI test overwrote them again). Confirm with product whether this final value is acceptable; if not, reset via PUT /api/auth/profile.
testuser.shop_name is currently 'دكان الاختبار v7' from an earlier v7 test run — was NOT part of iteration_7 changes but worth noting.
critical_code_review_comments: PUT /api/auth/profile correctly uses Depends(get_current_user) (not require_active_user) so locked owners can still edit — verified.
subscription-lock.tsx: onConfirm calls signOut() then router.replace('/sign-in') in a try/finally; a defensive useEffect also handles user===null by pushing to /sign-in. Good.
admin-profile.tsx: role guard runs in useEffect AND at render time (early-return spinner when user.role !== 'super_admin') so RBAC is solid on both direct navigation and after auth state resolves.
admin.tsx header now has two icon buttons (admin-profile-open + admin-signout); layout is a flex row so touch targets remain 44x44 — OK.

success_rate.frontend: code-review-only (UI automation aborted per Loop Prevention)

seed_data_creation: Seeded 9 extra customers for testuser (V7_LOCK_0..8) to force the lock screen, then deleted them via pymongo. Also created transient v7_* users inside pytest which are cleaned up in TestZZZRestore.test_cleanup_v7_users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: For UI Playwright: the sign-in form uses testIDs signin-username-input, signin-password-input, signin-submit-button. The subscription-lock ConfirmDialog exposes signout-lock-confirm (root) and signout-lock-confirm-confirm (confirm button). The admin dashboard has admin-profile-open in its header. The admin-profile screen exposes: admin-profile-screen, admin-profile-name, admin-profile-phone, admin-profile-info-save, admin-profile-info-success, admin-profile-info-error, admin-profile-pw-current, admin-profile-pw-new, admin-profile-pw-confirm, admin-profile-pw-save, admin-profile-pw-success, admin-profile-pw-error. When writing Python Playwright, avoid nested backslash escapes inside f-strings; use variables instead.
rca_of_issue: Not applicable — no bugs found in this iteration. Backend contract fully verified; frontend code review clean.
